# @zakkster/lite-headless/breadcrumb

Navigation trail (breadcrumb) showing the user's path through
hierarchical content. The last item is marked `aria-current="page"`
to indicate the current location; earlier items are ancestors
(typically links).

## Quick example

```js
import { createBreadcrumb } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/breadcrumb";

const bc = createBreadcrumb({
    onItemClick: (key, idx, event) => {
        // intercept for SPA routing if needed
        event.preventDefault();
        router.navigate(key);
    },
});

bc.attachRoot(navEl);
bc.attachList(olEl);
bc.attachItem(homeEl,      "home");
bc.attachItem(projectsEl,  "projects");
bc.attachItem(currentEl,   "this-project");  // auto-current (last)
```

## Options

| Option         | Default       | Notes                              |
| -------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `separator`    | `"/"`         | Default text for separators        |
| `onItemClick`  | -             | `(key, index, event) => void`      |

## Current item resolution

The last attached item is automatically the current. Override by:

- Passing `current: true` to `attachItem(el, key, {current: true})`
  (sticky -- stays until explicit setCurrent or another item is
  marked)
- Calling `bc.setCurrent("some-key")`
- Setting `current` attribute on `<lite-breadcrumb>`

`setCurrent(null)` clears the explicit choice; the marker falls
back to the last-attached item.

When the current item is detached, the marker shifts to the new
last-attached.

## ARIA

| Element     | Attributes                                                     |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Root        | `role="navigation"` · `aria-label="Breadcrumb"` (unless overridden) |
| List        | `role="list"`                                                  |
| Items       | `role="listitem"` · `aria-current="page"` on current · `data-current="true"` for CSS |
| Separator   | `aria-hidden="true"` · `data-bc-sep`                           |

Screen readers announce "navigation: Breadcrumb. List of 3 items.
Home, link. Projects, link. This project, current page."

## Separator handling

Two strategies, both supported:

**(a) Pure CSS** — use `::after` on items:
```css
[data-bc-item]:not(:last-child)::after {
    content: " / ";
    margin: 0 0.5rem;
    color: var(--text-faint);
    aria-hidden: true;       /* sadly not directly settable from CSS */
}
```
Note: CSS `aria-hidden` isn't a real thing — but generated content
is not in the accessibility tree, so screen readers won't read
`::after` content. This is the recommended approach.

**(b) Real DOM separators** — pass to `attachSeparator(el)`:
```html
<ol>
    <li data-bc-item="home"><a>Home</a></li>
    <span data-bc-sep></span>      <!-- aria-hidden="true" set by primitive -->
    <li data-bc-item="projects"><a>Projects</a></li>
    <span data-bc-sep></span>
    <li data-bc-item="current">Current</li>
</ol>
```

The wrapper auto-detects `[data-bc-sep]` and wires them.

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-breadcrumb current="projects">
    <ol data-bc-list>
        <li data-bc-item="home"><a href="/">Home</a></li>
        <li data-bc-item="projects"><a href="/projects">Projects</a></li>
        <li data-bc-item="this-project">This project</li>
    </ol>
</lite-breadcrumb>
```

### Attributes

| Attribute    | Effect                                       |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------- |
| `current`    | Reactive: key of current item                |
| `separator`  | Default separator text                       |

### Imperative API + events

```js
host.setCurrent(key)
host.current        // string | null
host.items          // [{ key, label, current }]

host.addEventListener("itemclick", e => e.detail.key);
```

## Common pattern: SPA navigation

```html
<lite-breadcrumb id="bc">
    <ol data-bc-list>
        <li data-bc-item="home"><a href="/">Home</a></li>
        <li data-bc-item="docs"><a href="/docs">Docs</a></li>
        <li data-bc-item="guide">Style Guide</li>
    </ol>
</lite-breadcrumb>

<script>
    bc.addEventListener("itemclick", (e) => {
        e.detail.originalEvent.preventDefault();
        myRouter.navigate(`/${e.detail.key}`);
    });
</script>
```

## When NOT to use

- Single-page apps with no navigable hierarchy — breadcrumb is for
  showing where the user IS, not for general navigation. Use the
  `menu` or `tabs` primitive for navigation actions.
- Step-by-step wizards — use `stepper`, which is semantically
  different (shows progress through a flow, not location in a tree).

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
